codeReview.summary: The publish/security checks are present in code (publish route enum validation, generate route rate limiting, no dangerous HTML rendering, and generic system prompt). However, this feature commit is not isolated: it bundles large unrelated UI/theme and artifact changes, so the feature-level review fails on scope and traceability.

issues.description: Feature commit e18c8c1 includes multi-user-landing validation artifacts from a different mission (`missionDir` points to fbcce583-... in this file). This is out of scope for publish-route/security work and makes the feature diff non-reviewable as an isolated unit.

The commit includes broad UI restyling changes unrelated to publish route/security validation (Tabs plus many dashboard/component files). Mixing unrelated functional surface area into this feature commit breaks change isolation and increases regression risk.
Generated TypeScript build output was committed. This should be excluded to reduce churn and avoid noisy diffs.

sharedStateObservations.observation: fullstack-worker skill does not enforce monorepo-scoped staging safeguards before commit.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: SKILL.md step 7 only says to commit. Transcript shows `git add -A .` and then broad staging/unstaging iterations before committing 79 files.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Conventions exist for path-scoped staging, but there is no shared enforcement mechanism to block unrelated/generated files.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Mission AGENTS.md requires scoped staging and excluding generated artifacts, yet commit e18c8c1 still includes `content-os/tsconfig.tsbuildinfo` and unrelated `.factory/validation/multi-user-landing/*` artifacts.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Monorepo git-root/pathspec context is still easy to miss during feature work.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows worker discovery step `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` => `/Users/anirudhmanjesh/hackathons` after staging confusion, indicating this operational context was not effectively surfaced early.

summary: Reviewed handoff, transcript skeleton, skill, and commit diff for e18c8c1. Security/publish behaviors are present in code, but the feature commit is contaminated with unrelated changes and artifacts, so review status is fail.
